class.name: ResonancePlayer
class.brief_description: A Steam Audio source that supports physics-based occlusion, transmission, and reverb.
class.description: ResonancePlayer extends AudioStreamPlayer3D for sources that interact with Steam Audio geometry. Assign a [url=#ResonancePlayerConfig]ResonancePlayerConfig[/url] to [member player_config] to enable occlusion, distance attenuation, air absorption, and directivity. When [member player_config] is null, behaves as a normal AudioStreamPlayer3D (no Resonance processing). [b]Polyphony:[/b] [code]max_polyphony[/code] (inherited from [AudioStreamPlayer3D]) may be > 1. With [member player_config] set, each overlapping voice runs the full Resonance pipeline; all voices share one Steam Audio source handle at this node’s position (same occlusion/reverb simulation). Reverb split output sums wet audio from every active voice. [method get_audio_instrumentation] aggregates per-voice counters and includes [code]polyphony_voice_count[/code] while playing. [b]Stream property:[/b] With [member player_config] set, [code]stream[/code] and [method get_stream] refer to your real [AudioStream] resource; the engine plays an internal wrapper. Changing [code]stream[/code] at runtime updates the wrapper without requiring a manual [method play] restart (existing playbacks may still decode the previous stream until stopped). Clearing [member player_config] restores the plain [AudioStreamPlayer3D] stream slot. [b]AudioStreamPlayer3D inspector (when [member player_config] is set):[/b] - [b]Works / applied in Resonance path:[/b] [code]volume_db[/code] / [code]volume_linear[/code] (applied in native [code]_mix[/code]), [code]max_db[/code] (ceiling combined with volume in native mix), [code]bus[/code], [code]pitch_scale[/code] (via engine [code]rate_scale[/code] to the decoder), [code]max_polyphony[/code], transform, [code]autoplay[/code], [code]play[/code] / [code]stop[/code] / [code]seek[/code]. - [b]Not used for distance like the default 3D player:[/b] [code]attenuation_model[/code], [code]unit_size[/code], [code]max_distance[/code] — [code]_ready[/code] sets attenuation to disabled; use [url=#ResonancePlayerConfig]ResonancePlayerConfig[/url] for distance and curves. - [b]Little or no Steam coupling:[/b] [code]emission_angle*[/code], [code]attenuation_filter*[/code], [code]panning_strength[/code] (Steam HRTF / directivity drive spatialization instead). [b]Reverb split:[/b] Child [code]ResonanceReverbOutput[/code] is a separate [AudioStreamPlayer]; set its [code]bus[/code] via [method set_reverb_split_output]. It does not automatically mirror the parent’s bus or mute — adjust both if your mix expects that. Inspector documentation for resource properties: open [url=#ResonancePlayerConfig]ResonancePlayerConfig[/url] from here (sub-resources have no documentation button in the inspector). [b]Scene tree:[/b] If this node is a [i]child[/i] of [ResonanceRuntime], Godot runs the player’s [code]_ready[/code] before the runtime’s [code]_ready[/code]. The extension retries registering the Steam source after the server initializes, so assigning [code]stream[/code] or calling [method play] / [method play_stream] in [code]_ready[/code] is fine. Prefer [method play_stream] in docs and templates; [method play] applies the same Resonance hookup (internal stream wrap, source handle, deferred simulation push, reverb split child). [b]AnimationPlayer:[/b] [code]Animation.TYPE_AUDIO[/code] ([b]Audio Clips[/b]) uses [code]AudioStreamPolyphonic[/code]. Returning a [code]ResonanceStreamPlayback[/code] wrapper for that stream crashes on Windows before playback starts; the extension therefore uses [b]native[/b] polyphonic playback for that case (stable, but [b]dry[/b] for Resonance). For Steam with Audio Clips, enable [member convert_animation_audio_tracks_at_runtime] on this node (converts tracks once at runtime to [method play_animation_audio_clip]), use [b]Call Method[/b] keys manually, or the editor tools under [b]Project → Tools → Nexus Resonance[/b].

method.name: play
method.description: Same as [method AudioStreamPlayer3D.play] for this class, plus when [member player_config] is set: wraps the stream in the internal Resonance playback path, ensures a Steam source handle exists (retries if [ResonanceServer] was not ready in [code]_ready[/code]), starts the reverb split child if present, and defers a playback-parameter push to the audio thread. Use this or [method play_stream]; both are supported.

method.name: play_stream
method.description: Calls [method play] with the same [code]from_position[/code]. Kept for existing projects and tutorials.

method.name: play_animation_audio_clip
method.description: Sets [code]stream[/code] and calls [method play] with [code]from_position[/code] (typically the clip’s start offset / trim). Intended for [AnimationPlayer] [b]method[/b] tracks (or after [member convert_animation_audio_tracks_at_runtime] rewrote [code]TYPE_AUDIO[/code]; see class description).

method.name: get_audio_instrumentation
method.description: Dropout/debug counters aggregated across all active polyphony voices when [member player_config] is set. Always includes a snapshot of selected Godot 3D player fields when [member player_config] is set: [code]godot_attenuation_model[/code], [code]godot_pitch_scale[/code], [code]godot_max_distance[/code], [code]godot_max_db[/code], [code]godot_unit_size[/code]. When at least one internal playback is active, also includes [code]polyphony_voice_count[/code] and the dropout counters. Empty dictionary when [member player_config] is null.

method.name: reset_audio_instrumentation
method.description: Resets instrumentation counters on every active internal playback for this node.

method.name: build_directivity_gizmo_lines
method.description: Returns a line list (pairs of [Vector3] vertices) describing a directivity wireframe gizmo in the player's local space (forward = [code]-Z[/code]). With [param enabled] and [param input_mode] = [code]0[/code] (Simulation Defined), draws the dipole polar curve [code]|(1 - weight) + weight * cos(theta)| ^ power[/code] as two meridians plus four azimuth rings. Otherwise draws a unit sphere (three great circles) plus a forward arrow; in User Defined mode ([param input_mode] = [code]1[/code]) the arrow is additionally scaled by [param user_value]. Used by both the editor gizmo and the runtime drawer so they stay visually identical; shape parameters typically come from the matching fields in [url=#ResonancePlayerConfig]ResonancePlayerConfig[/url].

members.member: [url=#ResonancePlayerConfig]ResonancePlayerConfig[/url] resource. When set, enables Steam Audio processing (occlusion, reverb, pathing, etc.). When null, acts as plain AudioStreamPlayer3D. All resource fields are documented on the linked page.

Pathing: Optional ResonanceProbeVolume to use for path finding. Empty = use first loaded volume with pathing. Only used when player_config is set. Note: Deleting a probe volume that is still referenced here can trigger a Godot engine error. Clear this property manually before deleting the volume if needed.
When true, this player is hidden from the debug overlay Audio Instrumentation list.

When [code]scene_type[/code] is Custom, registers every descendant [code]CollisionObject3D[/code] [RID] with [ResonanceServer] so Godot physics rays used for occlusion/reflections ignore those bodies. Disable if you intentionally want those colliders to block rays.

When [member player_config] is set and this is [code]true[/code] (runtime only): spawns a helper that converts [code]Animation.TYPE_AUDIO[/code] tracks on the current scene targeting [ResonancePlayer] into [b]method[/b] keys calling [method play_animation_audio_clip], once per scene load. Required for Steam with Audio Clips because native polyphonic playback cannot be wrapped (see class description).

Wireframe visualization of the emission direction (forward axis [code]-Z[/code]) and directivity pattern (dipole for Simulation Defined, unit sphere + arrow otherwise). Controls visibility in [b]both[/b] the editor Scene viewport and the running scene: [code]true[/code] = shown everywhere, [code]false[/code] = hidden everywhere. Shape derives from [member player_config] ([code]directivity_enabled[/code], [code]directivity_input[/code], [code]directivity_weight[/code], [code]directivity_power[/code], [code]directivity_value[/code]) and updates automatically when those fields change. Independent from the Debug HUD toggles.
